itemList.name: Potion of Healing
itemList.description:
A creature that drinks this magical red fluid regains 2d4 + 2 Hit Points.

Whatever its potency, the potion’s red liquid glimmers when agitated.

itemList.name: Arrows
itemList.description: Arrows are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

itemList.name: Rations
itemList.description: Rations consist of travel-ready food, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts. See “Malnutrition” for the risks of not eating.
itemList.effect: Sustains one creature for one day.

itemList.name: Torch
itemList.description: A Torch burns for 1 hour, casting Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. When you take the Attack action, you can attack with the Torch, using it as a Simple Melee weapon. On a hit, the target takes 1 Fire damage.
itemList.effect: Emits bright light in a 20 ft radius and dim light for an additional 20 ft for 1 hour.

itemList.name: Backpack
itemList.description: A Backpack holds up to 30 pounds within 1 cubic foot. It can also serve as a saddlebag.

itemList.name: Bedroll
itemList.description: A Bedroll sleeps one Small or Medium creature. While in a Bedroll, you automatically succeed on saving throws against extreme cold.

itemList.name: Blanket
itemList.description:
A warm blanket.

While wrapped in a blanket, you have Advantage on saving throws against extreme cold.
itemList.effect: Gives advantage on saving throws against extreme cold

itemList.name: Waterskin
itemList.description: A Waterskin holds up to 4 pints. If you don’t drink sufficient water, you risk dehydration.

itemList.name: Tinderbox
itemList.description: A Tinderbox is a small container holding flint, fire steel, and tinder (usually dry cloth soaked in light oil) used to kindle a fire. Using it to light a Candle, Lamp, Bullseye Lantern, Hooded Lantern, or Torch—or anything else with exposed fuel—takes a Bonus Action. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

itemList.name: Crowbar
itemList.description: Using a Crowbar gives you Advantage on Strength checks where the Crowbar’s leverage can be applied.
itemList.effect: Has advantage on Strength checks for leverage

itemList.name: Rope
itemList.description:
As a Utilize action, you can tie a knot with Rope if you succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check. The Rope can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check.

You can bind an unwilling creature with the Rope only if the creature has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. If the creature’s legs are bound, the creature has the Restrained condition until it escapes. Escaping the Rope requires the creature to make a successful DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action.
itemList.effect: Tie a knot or bind an unwilling creature if it has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition.

itemList.name: Grappling Hook
itemList.description: As a Utilize action, you can throw the Grappling Hook at a railing, a ledge, or another catch within 50 feet of yourself, and the hook catches on if you succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. If you tied a Rope to the hook, you can then climb it.

itemList.name: Healer's Kit
itemList.description: A Healer’s Kit has ten uses. As a Utilize action, you can expend one of its uses to stabilize an Unconscious creature that has 0 Hit Points without needing to make a Wisdom (Medicine) check.

itemList.name: Pouch
itemList.description: A Pouch holds up to 6 pounds within one-fifth of a cubic foot.

itemList.name: Sack
itemList.description: A Sack holds up to 30 pounds within 1 cubic foot.

itemList.name: Oil
itemList.description:
You can douse a creature, object, or space with Oil or use it as fuel, as detailed below.

Dousing a Creature or an Object. When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing an Oil flask. Target one creature or object within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be covered in oil. If the target takes Fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), the target takes an extra 5 Fire damage from burning oil.

Dousing a Space. You can take the Utilize action to pour an Oil flask on level ground to cover a 5-foot-square area within 5 feet of yourself. If lit, the oil burns until the end of the turn 2 rounds from when the oil was lit (or 12 seconds) and deals 5 Fire damage to any creature that enters the area or ends its turn there. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

Fuel. Oil serves as fuel for Lamps and Lanterns. Once lit, a flask of Oil burns for 6 hours in a Lamp or Lantern. That duration doesn’t need to be consecutive; you can extinguish the burning Oil (as a Utilize action) and rekindle it again until it has burned for a total of 6 hours.
itemList.effect: Douse a creature or space, or use as fuel for lamps and lanterns for 6 hours.

itemList.name: Flask
itemList.description: A Flask holds up to 1 pint.

itemList.name: Ink
itemList.description: Ink comes in a 1-ounce bottle, which provides enough ink to write about 500 pages.

itemList.name: Shovel
itemList.description: Working for 1 hour, you can use a Shovel to dig a hole that is 5 feet on each side in soil or similar material.

itemList.name: Holy Water
itemList.description: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Holy Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take 2d8 Radiant damage if it is a Fiend or an Undead.

itemList.name: Chain
itemList.description: As a Utilize action, you can wrap a Chain around an unwilling creature within 5 feet of yourself that has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition if you succeed on a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check. If the creature’s legs are bound, the creature has the Restrained condition until it escapes. Escaping the Chain requires the creature to make a successful DC 18 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check as an action. Bursting the Chain requires a successful DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check as an action.
itemList.effect: On fail: Inflicts a grapple, incapacited or restrained condition

itemList.name: Crossbow Bolts
itemList.description: Crossbow bolts are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

itemList.name: Club
itemList.description: A short staff or stick, usually made of wood.

itemList.name: Dagger
itemList.description: A knife with a very sharp point and usually two sharp edges.

itemList.name: Greatclub
itemList.description: A large heavy shaft, typically made of wood.

itemList.name: Handaxe
itemList.description: A wood composite handle with a sharp edged head (called a 'bit'), typically made of iron or steel.

itemList.name: Javelin
itemList.description: A javelin is a light spear designed primarily to be thrown.

itemList.name: Mace
itemList.description: A weapon with a heavy head on a solid shaft used to bludgeon opponents.

itemList.name: Quarterstaff
itemList.description: A staff of wood from 6 to 9 feet (about 2 to 3 m) long, used for attack and defense.

itemList.name: Light Hammer
itemList.description: The light hammer is a small sledge hammer, usually with a haft made of wood about one and a half feet long. Some have a pick on the back of the hammer head.

itemList.name: Sickle
itemList.description: A short-handled tool with a semicircular blade, typically used by farmers for cutting grain, lopping, or trimming.

itemList.name: Spear
itemList.description: A pole weapon consisting of a shaft, usually of wood, with a pointed head.

itemList.name: Shortbow
itemList.description: The shortbow is a flexible shaft of wood (or horn or bone) about 3 to 4 feet long with the ends connected by strong cord or a gut string that is a little shorter than the shaft, causing the wood to bend and keep the string under tension.

itemList.name: Light Crossbow
itemList.description: A ranged weapon that uses an elastic launching device consisting of a bow-like assembly mounted horizontally on a main frame.

itemList.name: Shortsword
itemList.description: A smaller version of the longsword, but longer than a dagger or dirk. It has a 12 to 20 inch, double-edged blade that ends in a sharp point. It has a cross-guard, grip, and pommel.

itemList.name: Longsword
itemList.description: A type of European sword characterized as having a cruciform hilt with a grip for two-handed use (6 to 12 inches). It has a straight, double-edged blade about 3 to 4 feet long.

itemList.name: Scimitar
itemList.description: A short sword with a curved blade that broadens toward the point.

itemList.name: Battleaxe
itemList.description: An axe head mounted on a wooden or metal haft about 2.5​ to ​3 feet long. Some battleaxes were known to have a short spike or blade on the back of their axeheads, as well as a spike on the end of their shafts. Some came with belt hooks on the side of their heads.

itemList.name: Greataxe
itemList.description: A large, heavy battleaxe with a double-bladed head. The shaft was usually constructed of stout wood.

itemList.name: Leather Armor
itemList.description:
The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.

Light Armor (1 Minute to Don or Doff).

If you wear Light armor and lack training with it, you have Disadvantage on any D20 Test that involves Strength or Dexterity, and you can’t cast spells.

itemList.name: Chain Shirt
itemList.description:
Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.

Medium Armor (5 Minutes to Don and 1 Minute to Doff).

If you wear Medium armor and lack training with it, you have Disadvantage on any D20 Test that involves Strength or Dexterity, and you can’t cast spells.

itemList.name: Breastplate
itemList.description:
This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

Medium Armor (5 Minutes to Don and 1 Minute to Doff).

If you wear Medium armor and lack training with it, you have Disadvantage on any D20 Test that involves Strength or Dexterity, and you can’t cast spells.

itemList.name: Chain Mail
itemList.description:
Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.

Heavy Armor (10 Minutes to Don and 5 Minutes to Doff)

If you wear Heavy armor and lack training with it, you have Disadvantage on any D20 Test that involves Strength or Dexterity, and you can’t cast spells.

itemList.name: Shield
itemList.description:
A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

Shield (Utilize Action to Don or Doff).
